stdout:
The prior namespace-ordering issue is fixed, but the new scaffold path still materializes rules without the required write lock. `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passes; `cargo test --workspace --quiet` still fails in this environment, but the actionable introduced issue is the unlocked scaffold path.

Review comment:

- [P2] Hold the init write lock while scaffolding rules — /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs:538-543
  When two `jit init` processes run concurrently in a repo where `.jit/rules.toml` is absent, both can pass this existence check and then write the same fixed temp paths for `rules.toml` and schemas without any lock held. That violates the hard requirement that `jit init` materializes defaults under the write lock, and can leave one init failing or producing a partially inconsistent scaffold. Please acquire an exclusive init/repo write lock around the absent-file check and all scaffold writes.
